name: Amadorio Reservoir Loop from Aigües
name_original: El Embalse del Amadorio — Circular desde Aigües por Orxeta
description: This moderate 27.8-mile mountain biking route circles the Amadorio Reservoir, offering diverse terrain and panoramic views near Aigües.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Aigües offers diverse landscapes, from mountainous backdrops to the Amadorio Reservoir, providing varied terrain for riders. The region is characterized by challenging climbs and rewarding descents, with panoramic views of the Costa Blanca. Its natural features include the Amadorio Reservoir, known for its clear waters, and elevated areas like Alto de Busot. This varied topography ensures engaging rides for all skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Aigües The most popular mountain bike trail is Amadorio Reservoir Loop from Aigües, a 27.8 miles (44.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 41 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views around the reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Amadorio Reservoir – Amadorio River Bridge loop from Aigües, a moderate 30.2 miles (48.6 km) path. This trail provides expansive views of the reservoir and surrounding natural features. Local mountain bikers also love the Aigües de Busot Preventorium – View of the Costa Blanca loop from Aigües, a 19.9 miles (32.0 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with views of the Costa Blanca, often completed in about 3 hours 4 minutes. Mountain biking around Aigües is defined by its mountainous backdrop, the Amadorio Reservoir, and diverse terrain. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy to difficult. The routes in Aigües are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 80 reviews. More than 1500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Aigües's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Aigües?
A: There are nearly 90 mountain bike trails around Aigües, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored over 1500 mountain biking adventures in the area.

Q: What are the general difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Aigües?
A: The trails in Aigües cater to various skill levels. You'll find 6 easy routes, 52 moderate routes, and 31 difficult routes. The terrain ranges from S0-S1 for easy rides to S3-S6 for more advanced routes that may require significant fitness and technical skill.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Aigües?
A: Yes, Aigües offers 6 easy mountain bike routes, which are generally classified as S0-S1 on the Single Track Scale. These trails are ideal for those new to mountain biking or looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What kind of challenges can advanced mountain bikers expect in Aigües?
A: Advanced mountain bikers will find 31 difficult routes in Aigües, often featuring challenging climbs and rewarding descents. These trails can include technical sections rated S3-S6, sometimes requiring advanced riding skills or even pushing the bike.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails in Aigües?
A: Many trails offer stunning views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4041337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amadorio Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, known for its clear, turquoise waters. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2838223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Busot&lt;/a&gt; mountain pass, offering impressive regional views, or the nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4631915" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Relleu Footbridge Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; with its spectacular gorge views.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Aigües?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Aigües are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e927508610/amadorio-reservoir-loop-from-aiguees" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amadorio Reservoir Loop from Aigües&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33746456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amadorio Reservoir – Amadorio River Bridge loop from Aigües&lt;/a&gt; also provides expansive views on a circular path.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Aigües?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 80 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse landscapes, the challenging climbs, rewarding descents, and the panoramic views of the Costa Blanca, especially around the Amadorio Reservoir.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that offer views of the Costa Blanca?
A: Absolutely. The region's mountainous backdrop often provides expansive views of the Costa Blanca. A notable route for this is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27645321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aigües de Busot Preventorium – View of the Costa Blanca loop from Aigües&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through mountainous terrain with stunning coastal vistas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Aigües?
A: Aigües, located in the Valencian Community, generally offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide mild temperatures, making them ideal. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended, while winters are usually mild.

Q: Are there any longer mountain bike routes for endurance riders?
A: Yes, Aigües has several longer routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33746456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amadorio Reservoir – Amadorio River Bridge loop from Aigües&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 30.2-mile (48.6 km) path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e927508610/amadorio-reservoir-loop-from-aiguees" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amadorio Reservoir Loop from Aigües&lt;/a&gt; covers 27.8 miles (44.7 km), both offering substantial riding time.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails in Aigües?
A: You can expect highly varied terrain, from the mountainous backdrop with challenging climbs and rewarding descents to trails around the Amadorio Reservoir. The region's diverse topography ensures engaging rides, often with panoramic views.
tips: For me, the most wooded and green area of Aigües begins here.
The reservoir can be circumnavigated very well by bike. The most beautiful view of the lake is from the dam wall.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 44.7 km long with an elevation gain of about 783 meters. For an average mountain biker, it's estimated to take around 3 hours and 40 minutes. However, this duration can vary significantly based on your fitness level, the pace you maintain, and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Is the El Embalse del Amadorio — Circular desde Aigües por Orxeta trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally considered to be of moderate difficulty for mountain biking, requiring good physical fitness. While some sections may be easier, other parts feature steep ascents and descents, particularly with loose rocks. Certain singletrack trails in the area are also rated as 'blue difficulty,' indicating a moderate physical demand. It might be challenging for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail, and what is the main starting point?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts from Aigües. There is a recreational area with parking near the Amadorio Dam, which makes for a convenient starting point. You can also begin directly from Aigües.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied, encompassing footpaths alongside the water, routes through forests, and some paved sections. You'll encounter verdant riverbeds, paths skirting the reservoir, and more challenging mountain trails with steep sections and elevation changes. Be prepared for mixed surfaces, including loose rocks, and you'll also pass through tunnels and ride across the impressive dam.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers beautiful panoramic views, including the majestic Aitana mountain range, the Serra d'Orxeta, and the rugged peaks around the Coll de Sacarest. A significant highlight is the Amadorio Reservoir itself, which offers views stretching to Villajoyosa and even a distant glimpse of Benidorm and the sea. You'll also pass through tunnels, some with built-in viewpoints, and ride across the impressive dam.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Given the Mediterranean climate, the best times to mountain bike this route are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder and more comfortable for physical activity. If riding in warmer months, it's advisable to carry plenty of water as natural sources are scarce.

faqs.question: Is the El Embalse del Amadorio — Circular desde Aigües por Orxeta route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, there is no specific information regarding dog restrictions. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other trail users, and to clean up after them. Always ensure your dog is fit enough for a moderate mountain biking trail.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to access this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the El Embalse del Amadorio — Circular desde Aigües por Orxeta mountain biking route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife and plant life might I encounter along the trail?
faqs.answer: The surrounding landscape features typical Mediterranean flora such as Aleppo pines, rosemary, esparto, and oleanders. You might also spot various wildlife, including herons, cormorants, frogs, turtles, eagles, kestrels, rabbits, squirrels, foxes, and even wild boar.

faqs.question: Does this mountain biking route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route partially overlaps with several other paths. You will find sections that are part of El Camí IT5TR2 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, Camino del Sureste - Ramal de Villajoyosa, Camino del Sureste - Ramal de Benidorm, Camí Vell d'Alcoi, and Camí Vell de Finestrat.

faqs.question: Is there water available on the trail, or should I bring my own?
faqs.answer: Natural water sources are scarce along the trail, especially during warmer months. It is highly advisable to carry sufficient water with you for the entire duration of your ride, particularly given the moderate difficulty and length of the route.
